stories.title: Hegseth faces questions about Iran in first congressional appearance since war began
stories.description: Making his first appearance before Congress since the Trump administration went to war against Iran, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced withering questioning from skeptical Democrats.

stories.title: 20,000 civilian sailors are stuck in the gulf that opens to the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Thousands of seafarers remain stuck as the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues. The United Nations urges for a plan to facilitate their release.

stories.title: Deadly Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon despite ceasefire
stories.description: Seventeen people - including two children - were killed, according to Lebanon's health ministry.

stories.title: Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: They are among nine people killed in successive attacks on Tuesday, which Israel's military says targeted a Hezbollah commander.

stories.title: Former US officials criticise Pentagon silence on deadly Iran school attack
stories.description: In the two months since the deadly strike, the US defence department has said only that the incident is under investigation.

stories.title: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
stories.description: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon amid ongoing ceasefire
stories.description: The US announced three days ago that the ceasefire was extended by three weeks after talks.

stories.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.description: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.title: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.title: Who is making decisions in Iran?
stories.description: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

stories.title: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.description: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries has been extended by three weeks after talks in Washington.

stories.title: US considers reducing troops in Germany

stories.title: At least 15 killed in southern Lebanon
stories.description: The US embassy in Beirut said Lebanon is at a critical crossroads and called for a meeting between Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, despite a fragile situation that many Lebanese would find hard to describe as a true ceasefire. stories.title: Deadline for US Congress on war in Iran
stories.description: The US President is reportedly considering new military strike options against Iran. stories.title: Opening Hormuz 'not a humanitarian gesture': Essential to keeping global food system operating
stories.description: Amid geopolitical volatility and economic fragility, François Picard is pleased to welcome John W.H. Denton, Secretary General of the International Chamber of Commerce. stories.title: Workers across the world march for peace and better pay in May Day rallies
stories.description: May Day rallies across the world brought workers out in force on Friday to protest rising energy prices caused by the US-Israeli war on Iran, with Turkish police arresting dozens of demonstrators trying to march to Taksim Square. stories.title: Middle East war live: Iran briefly activates Tehran air defences against ‘reconnaissance drones’
stories.description: Tehran's air defences were activated for around 20 minutes late Thursday "to counter small aircraft and reconnaissance drones", Tasnim and Fars news agencies reported. And the administration of US President Donald Trump signalled it would not be reined in by a looming deadline to...

stories.title: Jihadists urge united front against Mali junta as Bamako blockade begins
stories.description: Jihadist fighters in Mali have called for a broad uprising against the country’s military rulers, escalating tensions as they move to tighten pressure on the capital, Bamako. The appeal comes days after coordinated attacks on junta positions and coincides with the start of a bloc...

stories.title: Iran 'will not capitulate', conflict resolution expert says
stories.description: Iran's supreme leader declared on Thursday that the United States had suffered a shameful defeat, defiantly rejecting a warning from President Donald Trump that an economically punishing US naval blockade could be enforced for months to come. stories.title: Rallies under way as workers gather for International Labour Day
stories.description: Labour Day marches are being held as Iran war and rising energy prices threaten the global economy.

stories.title: Commercial flights from Tehran’s main airport resume amid cautious normalcy
stories.description: Flights are gradually back in the air, but uncertainty on the ground still lingers.

stories.title: Iran vows ‘long, painful’ response if US renews attacks
stories.description: Diplomatic efforts to resolve the conflict have hit an impasse, with a ceasefire in place since April 8.

stories.title: Why is piracy rising off Somalia again — and is the Iran war responsible?
stories.description: At least four vessels have been hijacked in the past week or two, raising fears of a piracy resurgence.

stories.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 63 as Trump signals possible attacks
stories.description: Iran’s president calls the US siege 'intolerable' as Donald Trump says war may resume.

stories.title: Trump backs FIFA letting Iran play at World Cup in US
stories.description: After months of campaigns and questions, FIFA says Iran will compete in the World Cup and matches will remain in the US.

stories.title: Iran war live: Tehran says US ports siege ‘intolerable’; Trump mulls action
stories.description: Iran’s president says US siege of Iranian ports amounts to an “extension of military operations” by Washington.

stories.title: US gas reaches $4.30 per gallon; Trump says prices will drop after Iran war
stories.description: Price of petrol in US jumps by nearly 30 cents in one week amid Strait of Hormuz blockade and Iran diplomatic deadlock.

stories.title: Trump's war powers deadline expires Friday as officials cite ceasefire loophole
stories.description: President Donald Trump faces a critical legal deadline on Friday that could determine the future of US military operations against Iran, even as his administration signals it may rely on a controversial legal interpretation to bypass congressional approval. stories.title: UN chief warns Hormuz closure ‘strangling’ global economy
stories.description: UNITED NATIONS: The escalating crisis in the Strait of Hormuz could push tens of millions into poverty, trigger a surge in global hunger and even tip the world toward recession, United Nations Secretary General Antonio Guterres warned on Thursday. stories.description: Amazon said on Thursday that restoring cloud computing operations in the United Arab Emirates (UAE), which were damaged in the conflict in the Middle East, is expected to take several months. stories.title: Mamdani calls on King Charles to return Koh-i-Noor diamond
stories.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani called for King Charles to “return” the prized Koh-i-Noor diamond, which the British Empire took from the Indian subcontinent in the 1800s, on the third day of the monarch’s state visit on Wednesday. stories.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE: Iran activates air defences as Trump faces congressional deadline
stories.description: Trump administration says its war in Iran has been ’terminated’ before 60-day deadline

stories.title: Hezbollah adopts new weapon: Fibre-optic drones, used widely in war in Ukraine
stories.description: Fibre-optic drones have a thin cable that connects an operator directly to the drone, making it impossible to electronically jam

stories.title: U.S. official says Iran war truce ‘terminated’ hostilities for war powers deadline
stories.description: There ⁠has been no exchange of fire between the ⁠U.S. armed forces and Iran since a fragile ceasefire began more than three weeks ago

stories.title: Israel-Iran war updates: Israel may have to 'act again' against Iran, Defence Minister Katz says
stories.description: Pakistan said that it was actively engaged with the U.S. and Iran to end the war in West Asia

stories.title: Lebanon says Israeli strikes kill 9 people, including 2 children
stories.description: Speaking to a delegation from the International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies, President Joseph Aoun had earlier slammed “continuing Israeli violations” in south Lebanon

stories.title: U.K. calls antisemitism an emergency as police investigate stabbing attack on 2 Jewish men
stories.description: Police have arrested a 45-year-old man on suspicion of attempted murder and labelled the stabbing attack as an act of terrorism. Detectives are working to determine a motive and whether there is any link to Iranian proxies
